name: The Doctor's Sourdough Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the sourdough starter and 2 1/2 cups of flour with all the water the night before baking.
2. Let it stand in a warm place overnight.
3. The next morning, mix butter into the warm milk until dissolved.
4. Stir in the yeast until fully dissolved.
5. Add honey and stir thoroughly.
6. Gradually add 2 more cups of flour, along with wheat germ.
7. Sprinkle sugar, salt, and baking soda over the mixture.
8. Gently press into the dough and mix lightly.
9. Allow to rise for 30 to 50 minutes until bubbly.
10. Add enough flour so that the dough cleanly coats the sides of a bowl.
11. Place the dough on a lightly floured board and knead 100 times or until smooth.
12. Form into four 1-pound loaves and place in well-greased loaf pans (9 x 3 inches).
13. Let rise until doubled, about 2 to 3 hours in a warm room.
14. Bake at 400 degrees F for 20 minutes.
15. Reduce the oven temperature to 325 degrees F and bake an additional 20 minutes or until thoroughly baked.
16. Remove from pans and place loaves on a rack to cool.
17. Butter the tops of the loaves to prevent a hard crust.
Makes 4 1-pound Loaves
